{"id":"mxdx-vuy.2","title":"Plan Task 8: P2PTransport adapter with E2EE, peer verification, idle timeout","description":"Plan Task 8: P2PTransport adapter with E2EE, peer verification, idle timeout

**Files:**
- Create: packages/core/p2p-transport.js
- Create: packages/e2e-tests/test/p2p-transport.test.js

**Requirements:**
- Static factory: P2PTransport.create({ matrixClient, encryptFn, decryptFn, signFn, verifySignatureFn, localDeviceId, ... })
- sendEvent(roomId, type, content) — encrypts via encryptFn, routes via data channel or falls back to Matrix. NEVER sends unencrypted terminal data over the data channel.
- onRoomEvent(roomId, type, timeoutSecs) — checks P2P inbox (decrypts via decryptFn), falls through to Matrix polling
- setDataChannel(channel) — connects WebRTC channel, initiates peer verification, starts keepalive + idle timer after verification
- Peer verification: challenge-response with Ed25519 device key signatures. 10s timeout. Failure → close channel, fall back to Matrix
- Maximum frame size: 64KB. Drop and log oversized incoming frames before JSON.parse()
- Idle timer: resets on send/receive, fires onHangup('idle_timeout') when expired
- Reconnect with exponential backoff: 10s → 20s → 40s → 80s → 160s → 300s (5 min cap). Resets after successful P2P session
- Application-level acks: tracks pending, requeues unacked via Matrix on channel close
- Keepalive: ping every 15s, pong timeout 5s
- In-band frame types: encrypted, ack, ping, pong, peer_verify
- TDD: 8+ tests covering Matrix fallback, E2EE, pre-verification blocking, P2P delivery, unacked requeue, oversized frame drop, idle timeout, backoff

**Security:** E2EE mandatory — terminal data is Megolm-encrypted before placement on data channel. Peer identity verified via challenge-response after channel opens. Frame size limits prevent DoS. See docs/reviews/security/2026-03-10-p2p-transport-plan.md.
